title: Loop specification
description: A loop the loopengine runtime can execute. A valid loop has a trigger (cadence), a verifier, external state, and brakes/escalation.

id.description: Unique loop id; keys its state section.

kind.description: Loop type. git-commit-triage: read-only triage (L1). assisted-fix: propose a verified change on a branch in an isolated worktree (L2, no auto-merge).

task.description: assisted-fix only: the change to make, in plain language.

test_command.description: assisted-fix only: ground-truth verifier run in the worktree (exit 0 = pass).

maker_command.description: assisted-fix only: shell command run in the worktree to produce the change (the agent-step placeholder). Omitted when a maker callable is supplied programmatically.

cadence.description: Human-readable trigger cadence (e.g. '1d'). Enforced by the scheduler, not the runtime.

state_file.description: Path to the JSON state file.

repo.description: Absolute path to the target git repo.

refs.description: git-commit-triage: branches/refs to watch. If 2+, ahead/behind divergence is reported.

first_run_since.description: Lookback window for the first triage run (no cursor yet).

base_ref.description: assisted-fix: ref to branch the worktree off.

budget.description: Hard brakes (enforced by the Rust core, mirrored in Python).
